trim.editor.description: After import, the same main card shows the preview, timeline, trim range, and export settings.
trim.placeholder.title: Import a file or drag it here to start trimming
trim.placeholder.description: After import, the same main card shows the file preview, playback controls, timeline, dual-thumb trim range, and output format selection.
trim.preview.failed.audioUnavailable: This audio file can't be previewed right now, but you can still try exporting it directly.
trim.preview.failed.videoUnavailable: This video can't be previewed right now, but you can still try exporting it directly.
trim.preview.failed.fileUnavailable: This file can't be previewed right now.
trim.preview.failed.checkFile: This file can't be previewed. Check the file and try again.
trim.preview.failed.importDetails: The file import failed. Check the detailed error information below.
trim.selection.summaryPlaceholder: Import a file to set the trim range.
trim.selection.rule.fast: Speed priority reuses the original streams when the container stays compatible, which exports faster but may drift slightly from the preview around non-keyframe boundaries.
trim.selection.rule.accurate: Accuracy priority exports strictly between the selected start and end points. For video, smart trim is tried first and falls back to full precise re-encoding when needed.
trim.settings.outputDirectoryHint: Leave empty to export beside the source file
trim.strategy.option.fast.description: Keeps the current fast export path: if the input and output containers are compatible, the original streams are reused first for faster results.
trim.strategy.option.accurate.descriptio: Always uses the precise trim path. For video, smart trim is tried first and falls back to full precise re-encoding when needed.
trim.strategy.option.accurate.gpuDescrip: Always uses input-side `-ss/-t` precise trimming. Video tries smart trim first, then picks encoding in this order: NVIDIA NVENC -> AMD AMF -> Intel Quick Sync -> CPU.
trim.strategy.option.accurate.cpuDescrip: Always uses input-side `-ss/-t` precise trimming. Video tries smart trim first, and if re-encoding is needed, the CPU handles the full fallback path.
trim.status.ready: Import an audio or video file, or drag one here to start trimming.
trim.status.busy: A clip is being exported right now. Wait for it to finish or cancel it first.
trim.status.hasInputAlready: A file is already loaded. Remove it before importing another one.
trim.status.importSuccess: {fileName} was imported. Drag the start and end points to confirm the trim range.
trim.status.outputDirectoryUpdated: The trim output folder was set to: {path}
trim.status.outputDirectoryCleared: The trim output folder was cleared. Exports will use the source file's folder.
trim.status.invalidSelection: The current trim range is invalid. Adjust the start and end points.
trim.status.exportPreparing: Preparing to export the trimmed clip...
trim.status.gpuFallbackRetry: GPU encoding failed, so the export fell back to a CPU retry automatically.
trim.status.exportException: An exception occurred during trim export.
trim.progress.detail.preparing: Validating the range and preparing FFmpeg export arguments...
trim.progress.detail.unknown: Exporting the trimmed clip. Progress is not available yet.
trim.import.rejected.noInput: No files were provided for import.
trim.import.rejected.singleItemOnly: The trim workspace can import only one file at a time.
trim.import.rejected.singleFileOnly: The trim workspace supports a single audio or video file only. Folders are not supported.
trim.import.rejected.unsupportedType: This file type is not supported in the trim workspace.
trim.import.rejected.singleVideoOnly: The trim workspace can import only one video file at a time.
trim.import.rejected.singleVideoFileOnly: The trim workspace supports a single video file only. Folders are not supported.
trim.import.failure.unknownException: An unknown exception occurred while importing the file.
trim.import.failure.noDiagnosticDetails: No additional diagnostic details were captured.
trim.import.failure.noTrimmableStreams: The current file does not contain trimmable audio or video streams.
trim.import.failure.invalidDuration: The current file has an invalid duration and can't be trimmed.
trim.import.failure.cannotParseVideoFile: Couldn't parse the current video file.
trim.import.failure.noVideoStream: The current file does not contain a trimmable video stream.
trim.import.failure.invalidVideoDuration: The current video has an invalid duration and can't be trimmed.
trim.export.failure.noFfmpegMessage: FFmpeg did not return a usable error message.
trim.export.transcoding.audio.fastPath: The source and target output are compatible, so the original audio stream was reused first.
trim.export.transcoding.audio.compatibil: The source and target output were not fully compatible, so the export fell back to compatible audio transcoding.
trim.export.transcoding.video.fastPath: Speed priority matched a compatible container, so the clip was exported by reusing the original streams first.
trim.export.transcoding.video.fastFallba: Speed priority could not fully reuse the original streams for this clip, so the export fell back to compatible re-encoding automatically.
trim.export.transcoding.video.accurateCo: Accuracy priority completed a precise trim using the selected start and end points.
trim.export.transcoding.video.accurateCp: Under accuracy priority, GPU encoding failed, so the precise trim fell back to CPU reprocessing automatically.
trim.smartTrim.fallback.noKeyframeRange: The selected clip did not cover a sufficient keyframe range, so it fell back to full precise re-encoding.
trim.smartTrim.fallback.unstable: Smart trim could not complete reliably, so it fell back to full precise re-encoding.
trim.smartTrim.fallback.shortSelection: The selected clip is short, so full precise re-encoding is more stable and was used automatically.
trim.smartTrim.fallback.outputUnsupporte: The current output format is not suitable for smart trim, so it fell back to full precise re-encoding.
trim.smartTrim.fallback.codecUnsupported: The current video codec is not suitable for smart trim, so it fell back to full precise re-encoding.
trim.smartTrim.result.completed: Accuracy priority used smart trim: the head and tail were re-encoded precisely while the middle keyframe range was copied directly.
trim.smartTrim.result.completedCpuFallba: Accuracy priority used smart trim and automatically fell back to CPU export when GPU encoding was unavailable.
trim.log.boundaryWarmupFailed: Trim boundary warmup failed and the current preview state was kept.
